evals.prompt: Run /news on Bloomberg Technology. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, browse the web, or run any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the SQL/search queries you'd run, the candidate table format you'd present, and the vault modifications you'd make. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript covering movers cross-check, analyst scan, source discovery with date gate, candidate table, ingestion plan with entity linking, earnings check, downstream impact, and compliance

assertions.name: phase0_movers_crosscheck
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a major movers cross-check (searching for stocks moving ±8% or more) BEFORE looking at any articles?

assertions.name: phase0_vault_crossref
assertions.prompt: Does the agent cross-reference movers against vault actor notes?

assertions.name: phase05_analyst_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent search for at least 5 of the 8 named analysts (Croft, Currie, Kaneva, Blanch, Alden, Pozsar, Yardeni, Wilson)?

assertions.name: phase05_search_patterns
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use specific search patterns for analyst searches (not just generic mentions)?

assertions.name: date_gate
assertions.prompt: Does the agent apply a 48-hour date gate to all candidate articles?

assertions.name: vault_coverage_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check each candidate against existing vault coverage before presenting?

assertions.name: candidate_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent present a numbered candidate table with columns for #, Article, Date, and Status?

assertions.name: user_selection_wait
assertions.prompt: Does the agent explicitly wait for user selection before proceeding to ingestion?

assertions.name: source_urls
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to add source URLs to the daily note's Sources section?

assertions.name: publication_date
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use article publication date (not ingestion date) for daily note placement?

assertions.name: check_before_create
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to run check_before_create.py before creating any new notes?

assertions.name: phase3_earnings
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 3 earnings calendar check?

assertions.name: phase4_downstream
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 4 downstream impact check with second-order effects?

assertions.name: compliance_check
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to run check_note_compliance.py on modified notes?

assertions.name: copyright_rule
assertions.prompt: Does the agent respect the copyright rule — planning to use short quotes only (<15 words)?

evals.prompt: I want to do a /news sweep focused on energy and oil. Check Reuters and FT for the last 48 hours. I'm particularly interested in anything about Iran sanctions, OPEC+, or nuclear power demand from AI datacenters. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, browse the web, or run any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the SQL/search queries you'd run, the candidate table format you'd present, and the vault modifications you'd make. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript with energy-sector movers check, energy analyst prioritization, dual-source date gate, concept note plans for Iran/OPEC/nuclear, cross-vault flagging, downstream impact

assertions.name: phase0_energy_movers
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 0 major movers cross-check focused on energy sector stocks?

assertions.name: phase05_energy_analysts
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check all 8 analysts and prioritize energy analysts (Croft, Currie, Blanch, Kaneva) over equity strategists?

assertions.name: date_gate_dual_source
assertions.prompt: Does the agent apply a 48-hour date gate to both Reuters and FT?

assertions.name: candidate_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent present a numbered candidate table with date and vault status columns?

assertions.name: concept_notes
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to update or check for concept notes on Iran sanctions, OPEC+, and nuclear power?

assertions.name: cross_vault_geopolitics
assertions.prompt: Does the agent consider cross-vault linking to the geopolitics vault for Iran/sanctions content?

assertions.name: entity_linking
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan entity linking with wikilinks for all mentioned entities?

assertions.name: phase3_earnings
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 3 earnings calendar check for energy companies?

assertions.name: phase4_downstream
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 4 downstream impact check identifying second-order effects (e.g., refinery margins, shipping rates, LNG)?

assertions.name: publication_date
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to use article publication date for daily note placement?

evals.prompt: Big day in markets — semiconductors are getting crushed. NVDA down 7%, ASML down 9%, AMD down 5%. Run /news to figure out what's driving this and update the vault. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, browse the web, or run any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the SQL/search queries you'd run, the candidate table format you'd present, and the vault modifications you'd make. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Full phased transcript with ASML flagged as ≥8% mover, catalyst hypothesis tree, subsector dispersion analysis, event note creation, cross-vault flagging for export controls

assertions.name: phase0_asml_flagged
assertions.prompt: Does the agent flag ASML as a major mover (≥8%) requiring vault actor update?

assertions.name: phase0_nvda_amd
assertions.prompt: Does the agent also flag NVDA and AMD even though below 8% — they are vault actors with significant moves?

assertions.name: catalyst_search
assertions.prompt: Does the agent search for the specific catalyst driving the semiconductor selloff?

assertions.name: subsector_dispersion
assertions.prompt: Does the agent present subsector dispersion in the daily note (which names hit hardest vs held up)?

assertions.name: phase05_analysts
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check Mike Wilson and Ed Yardeni (plus others) for macro/equity context?

assertions.name: date_gate
assertions.prompt: Does the agent apply a date gate to all source candidates?

assertions.name: candidate_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent present a numbered candidate table before ingesting?

assertions.name: actor_note_updates
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to update semiconductor-related actor notes (NVDA, ASML, AMD at minimum)?

assertions.name: event_note
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to create a dedicated event note since the selloff touches 3+ actors?

assertions.name: cross_vault
assertions.prompt: Does the agent consider whether this belongs in both investing and geopolitics vaults (if export controls)?

assertions.name: phase3_earnings
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check the earnings calendar — did any semi company report?

assertions.name: phase4_downstream
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a Phase 4 downstream impact check?

evals.prompt: Anything interesting happen in markets today? Check if there's stuff I should update in the vault. Today is March 17, 2026. This is a dry run — do NOT actually modify the vault, browse the web, or run any tools. Do NOT read any skill files from .claude/skills/ — work only from CLAUDE.md and your general knowledge. Instead, produce a detailed transcript of exactly what you would do at each phase, including the SQL/search queries you'd run, the candidate table format you'd present, and the vault modifications you'd make. Show your full reasoning for each decision.
evals.expected_output: Agent should still perform full pipeline despite vague prompt: movers cross-check, analyst scan, source discovery, candidate table, earnings check, downstream impact

assertions.name: phase0_movers
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a systematic major movers cross-check (searching for stocks moving ±8% or more)?

assertions.name: phase05_analysts
assertions.prompt: Does the agent search for at least 5 of the 8 named analysts?

assertions.name: source_discovery
assertions.prompt: Does the agent identify and scan at least one news source (Bloomberg, Reuters, FT, WSJ)?

assertions.name: date_gate
assertions.prompt: Does the agent apply a 48-hour date gate to candidate articles?

assertions.name: candidate_table
assertions.prompt: Does the agent present a numbered candidate table before ingesting?

assertions.name: phase3_earnings
assertions.prompt: Does the agent check the earnings calendar?

assertions.name: phase4_downstream
assertions.prompt: Does the agent perform a downstream impact check?

assertions.name: copyright_rule
assertions.prompt: Does the agent plan to use short quotes only (<15 words)?

assertions.name: publication_date
assertions.prompt: Does the agent use article publication date (not ingestion date) for daily note placement?

assertions.name: structured_phases
assertions.prompt: Does the agent follow a structured multi-phase approach (not just ad-hoc browsing)?
